item.title: High alert on border
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh and the Bangladesh Coast Guard are on high alert in bordering areas as the Indian Border Security Force has continued its attempts to push more people into Bangladesh illegally....

item.title: Protecting plants, protecting humanity
item.description: WHEN politicians speak about infrastructure, they usually mean roads, bridges, ports, airports, power plants and digital networks. Economists measure growth through factories, investment flows, exports and productivity. Technology enthusiasts imagine a future built......

item.title: Measles outbreak and national preparedness
Carbon finance requires credibility
item.description: BANGLADESH is not a major contributor to climate change. Its share of global greenhouse-gas emissions is only about 0.4 per cent, yet it remains among the countries most exposed to floods, cyclones, salinity intrusion and other climate shocks. This imbalance creates a strong......

item.title: Opportunities for agriculture
item.description: RICE-BASED agriculture is the backbone of food security. Among the three major rice varieties, aman occupies a special importance because of its extensive coverage during the monsoon season and its contribution to national rice production. After harvesting boro in......

item.title: Weak urban management fuels dengue risk
item.description: THE monsoon season is about to set in with warning signs of a preventable public health crisis as official data show persistently high Aedes mosquito density across most of Dhaka’s south. A pre-monsoon survey of the Dhaka South City Corporation conducted in......

item.title: BNP should start practising in-party democracy
item.description: THE Bangladesh Nationalist Party’s prolonged failure to hold regular councils has created a democratic deficit within the organisation, frustrating grass-roots activists and concentrating decision-making power in the hands of a small leadership circle. The party’s national council......

item.title: Gambhir tells struggling Pant to play natural game in Afghan Test
item.description: India coach Gautam Gambhir told struggling Rishabh Pant on Friday to stick to his natural attacking game after removing him as vice-captain for the one-off Test against Afghanistan....

item.title: Uzbekistan’s debut WC crowns surge in football popularity
item.description: With its team already hailed as heroes and under the star guidance of Ballon d’Or and World Cup winner Fabio Cannavaro, Uzbekistan will make its first appearance at the 2026 tournament with no pressure from fans back home....

item.title: Spurs sign former Liverpool defender Robertson
item.description: Tottenham signed former Liverpool defender Andy Robertson on a free transfer on Friday, ending the Scotland captain’s nine-year spell at Anfield....

item.title: Men finish sixth in U18 Asia Cup hockey
item.description: Bangladesh men’s hockey team finished sixth in the Under-18 Asia Cup after a 2-1 defeat to South Korea in the place-deciding match at the Kawasaki Heavy Industries Hockey Stadium in Kakamigahara, Japan on Friday. ...

item.title: Mushfiq, Hridoy tons headline rain-hit day
item.description: Mushfiqur Rahim and Towhid Hridoy’s blazing hundreds for the Mohammedan Sporting Club against the Gazi Group Cricketers were the highlights on Friday, before rain forced five Dhaka Premier Division Cricket League ninth-round matches move to reserve day....

item.title: Bangladesh’s hat-trick or India’s hexa!
item.description: Bangladesh will be looking to secure their third consecutive title of the SAFF Women’s Championship when they take on the five-time champions and hosts India in the final of the tournament at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Goa today....

item.title: Nearly 1.5m displaced in Haiti: UN
item.description: Some 1.47 million people are displaced in gang-ravaged Haiti, the United Nations’ migration agency said Friday, warning that its ability to help could dry up within months....

item.title: Nigeria repatriating over 1,000 nationals from S Africa
item.description: Nigeria is planning to repatriate more than 1,000 of its nationals from South Africa as anti-immigrant tensions rise in the country, long a destination for documented and undocumented African workers....

item.title: Ireland bans two Israeli ministers from entering country
item.description: Ireland announced on Friday it has banned Israeli national security minister Itamar Ben Gvir and finance minister Bezalel Smotrich from entering the country, slamming their behaviour towards pro-Palestinian activists....

item.title: Journalist jailed in Tehran pleas for US help
item.description: An Iranian-American journalist sentenced to 10 years in a notorious Tehran prison called on Washington to provide medical help for him and other US detainees, CBS News reported Friday....

item.title: Andrew sublet royal cottages, UK auditors reveal
item.description: Britain’s disgraced former prince Andrew earned a private income from subletting cottages while paying a symbolic ‘peppercorn rent’ for a mansion for over two decades, government auditors revealed Friday....

item.title: 62pc students want 5pc GDP allocation for edn: survey
item.description: At least 62 per cent of university students in Bangladesh have called for increasing the education sector’s allocation to at least 5 per cent of the country’s gross domestic product, according to a survey by the Education Rights Parliament....

item.title: Rainfall down 34pc in Rajshahi, water security at risk: study
item.description: Monsoon rainfall in Rajshahi has declined by about 34 per cent over the past five decades, while temperatures have continued to rise, deepening water stress across the district and threatening agriculture, livelihoods and long-term water security, according to a recent study....

item.title: Prime accused held in Khulna triple murder case
item.description: Members of Rapid Action Battalion arrested the prime accused in the triple murder case involving the killing of a woman and her two grandchildren in Khulna’s Sonadanga area....

item.title: Navy detains 52 ‘smugglers’, seizes cement, fishing boats
item.description: The Bangladesh Navy has detained 52 suspected smugglers and seized 1,450 bags of cement and fishing boats during an operation against cross-border smuggling in the Bay of Bengal in Chattogram....

item.title: State minister calls for global cooperation to build habitable world
item.description: State minister for environment, forests and climate change Shaikh Faridul Islam has called on the member states of the Global Environmental Facility to work together to build a habitable world for present and future generations....

item.title: Three, including 2 children, drown in Rangpur
item.description: Three people, including two children, died in two separate drowning incidents in different parts of Rangpur on Friday....

item.title: Man, son killed from electrocution in Habiganj
item.description: A United Arab Emirate returnee and his son died from electrocution at Bahubal upazila in Habiganj on Friday noon....

item.title: Four AL activists arrested in Sirajganj
item.description: The police have arrested four activists of Awami League, the activities of which are now banned, and its affiliated organisations in Sirajganj sadar upazila in connection with separate murder and explosives cases....

item.title: Zia Memorial Museum in Ctg to be reopened after renovation: minister
item.description: Liberation war affairs minister Ahmed Azam Khan on Friday said that the Zia Memorial Museum beside Chattogram Circuit House would be renovated and reopened to visitors soon....

item.title: Wet spell eases heatwave, lightning strikes kill 4
item.description: Heatwave conditions eased across most parts of the country following a spell of rain and thunderstorms from Thursday night....

item.title: AL activists stage demos in Ctg
item.description: Leaders and activists of the Awami League and its affiliated organisations, whose activities are banned, brought out separate processions in three areas of Chattogram city on Friday morning....

item.title: Lebanese leaders rebuke Iran as Israel, Hezbollah trade attacks
item.description: Lebanon’s leaders issued pointed calls for Iran to stop interfering in their country’s affairs on Friday, as Israel and the Tehran-backed Hezbollah traded attacks after a new truce deal collapsed before it even began....

item.title: Indian next envoy to Bangladesh receives LoC
item.description: Indian high commissioner-designate to Bangladesh Dinesh Trivedi on Friday received the Letters of Credence for his appointment as the high commissioner of India to the People’s Republic of Bangladesh from president of India Droupadi Murmu....

item.title: Residents of two Tangail villages clash again ignoring Section 144
item.description: Fresh clashes broke out on Friday, in violation of Section 144 imposed at two villages in Tangail, after deadly clashes between residents of the villages on Thursday left one person dead and at least 15 others injured....

item.title: Digital campaign against govt risks democracy: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Friday alleged that a section of people are engaged in various conspiracies aimed at preventing democracy from taking root in Bangladesh....

item.title: Man held for attacking ex-wife with acid in Habiganj
item.description: A man was arrested on charge of throwing acid on the face of his divorced wife at sadar upazila in Habiganj on Thursday night....

item.title: Bhola human chain urges govt to save water bodies
item.description: Speakers at a human chain in Bhola on Friday call for the eviction of all illegal encroachments from natural water bodies, strict enforcement of environmental and land-use laws, and full restoration of natural water flows....

item.title: Schools, technical instts to resume classes tomorrow
item.description: All government and non-government primary and secondary level schools, as well as technical and vocational institutions nationwide, will resume academic activities tomorrow following the combined Eid-ul-Azha and summer vacation....

item.title: Zverev to face Cobolli in French Open final after beating Mensik
item.description: Alexander Zverev moved to the verge of a long-awaited first Grand Slam title as the second seed saw off Jakub Mensik in four sets on Friday to set up a French Open final against Flavio Cobolli....

item.title: Atkinson double leaves New Zealand reeling
item.description:
Gus Atkinson struck twice late on the second day as England pressed for victory in the first Test against New Zealand at Lord's on Friday.
...

item.title: Yamal named La Liga player of the year
item.description: Teenage Barcelona striker Lamine Yamal was named player of the year in la Liga on Friday after spearheading the club's drive to the title....

item.title: Fire at Mirpur market brought under control, no casualties
item.description: A fire that broke out in the capital’s Purabi market in Mirpur-11 on Friday morning was brought under control and doused within an hour with the efforts of four firefighting units....

item.title: White Paper hosts art camp at historic Birulia Zamidar Bari
item.description: The art organisation White Paper organised a day-long art camp at the historic Birulia Zamidar Bari at Savar in Dhaka on Friday, aiming to strengthen the mutual exchange of ideas between artists and bridge the connection between contemporary art and heritage sites....

item.title: 95 shops burnt to ashes in Kurigram fire
item.description: A massive fire burnt 95 shops to ashes at a bazar in Bhurungamari upazila headquarters of Kurigram Friday morning....

item.title: Activists demand independent art practices, safety in marginalised areas
item.description: Filmmakers, film organisers, and political leaders joined activists in Dhaka on Friday, demanding independent art practices and cultural safety in marginalised areas during a protest rally and human chain....

item.title: FIFA ups payments to clubs who send players to World Cup
item.description: Clubs whose players go to the World Cup, or appeared in qualifying, will receive increased compensation this summer, FIFA, the governing body of world football, announced on Friday....

item.title: Protests against power price hike continue
item.description: Different political parties and civil society organisations on Friday continued protests against the government›s decision to increase the retail power price....

item.title: Sirajganj crumbling road leaves thousands struggling
item.description: Residents of several villages in Ullapara upazila of Sirajganj have been enduring severe hardship due to the dilapidated condition of a key rural road that has remained largely neglected for nearly two decades....

item.title: Humayun Ahmed captured hearts of middle-class families: discussion
item.description: Speakers on Friday, including researchers, filmmakers, and educationists, remarked that writer-filmmaker Humayun Ahmed successfully entered the homes and hearts of middle-class families through his creative works....

item.title: NCP unveils Tk 8.52 lakh crore shadow budget for FY27
item.description: The National Citizen Party on Friday unveiled a ‘shadow budget’ for the 2026–27 financial year, proposing a Tk 8,52,157 crore spending plan focused on economic reform, job creation, revenue mobilisation and expanded social protection....

item.title: Topu brace gives Bangladesh historic win over San Marino
item.description:
Veteran defender Topu Barman scored a brace as Bangladesh earned a 2-1 win over San Marino in their first-ever international match on European soil at the San Marino Stadium in Serravalle on Friday night.
...

item.title: 11 killed in road accidents in five districts
item.description: At least 11 people were killed in six incidents of road accidents in five districts on Friday....

item.title: SAU holds tree plantation programme
item.description: Sher-e-Bangla Agricultural University held a tree plantation programme, marking World Environment Day, on the university premises in Dhaka on Friday....

item.title: US Senate approves $70b for immigration crackdown
item.description: The US Senate on Friday approved $70 billion in funding for Donald Trump’s hardline immigration crackdown, but only after a long day of votes on multiple amendments that highlighted Republican infighting over some of the president’s other contentious policy proposals....

item.title: ‘Cockroach Party’ chief flies to New Delhi for protests
item.description: The founder of India’s satirical online ‘Cockroach People’s Party’ said he was flying back to New Delhi on Friday, to take the viral social media campaign to the streets....

item.title: Iran fires warning missiles at US destroyers
item.description: Iran’s military said on Friday it had fired ‘warning missiles’ at two US destroyers in the Gulf of Oman, forcing the vessels to leave the area, according to state media....

item.title: HSIA cargo village fire under control
item.description: The fire broke out in a cargo area next to the Gate-9 of the HSIA, said Fire Service and Civil Defence headquarters deputy assistant director Shahjahan Sikder......

item.title: Iran dismisses idea of Trump meeting supreme leader
item.description: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi has dismissed the idea of his country’s supreme leader meeting Donald Trump, after the US president floated the prospect....

item.title: India claims following due process in push-ins
item.description: New Delhi on Friday claimed that it followed due process in repatriations from India amid allegations against the country’s Border Security Force of recent push-in attempts....

item.title: AD-DIN NEWBORN DEATHS: Families now support exhumation for justice
item.description: The families of the newborns who died at Ad-din Medical College Hospital in the capital on May 27 have said that they will support exhuming the bodies of their babies if required for the sake of justice....

item.title: 192 influential people in jail
item.description: At least 192 political leaders and influential figures from different professions, all linked to Awami League politics, are currently detained in jails in cases of crimes against humanity relating to July 2024 mass uprising....
